body:
Fixes two problems at the upper boundary and with tracer-mass consistency
* Ensures zero mass flux through top boundary even in the presence of warped coordinate surfaces (before the flux would only be zero if top layers are level)
* Ensures tracer-mass consistency in EDMF by treating all tracers (e.g., condensates) like `mse` and `q_tot`, in difference form, with corresponding Jacobian updates. Reduces SGS mass-flux tendency with an artificially uniform `q_lcl` from 1.1e-7 to 6.7e-14 (Float32 roundoff).
title: Fixes problems near upper boundary and tracer-mass continuity

## Purpose 
Update CTS:
Route enforce_physical_constraints! through ClimaTimeSteppers' new `constrain_state!` hook (per-step by default) and drop the separate callback. Frequencies are configurable via `update_cache_every` / `update_constrain_state_every`. This avoids redundant cache calls.

title: Update CTS

title: Remove GHA tests on Windows

body:
## Fix subsidence top BC in cases with prescribed forcing (GCM- or ERA-driven)

* Replace `ᶜsubdivᵥ` (top extrapolatation, which copied the cell below into the
top cell) with `ᶜadvdivᵥ` in the advective-form subsidence operator. With
zero boundary fluxes on both divergences, the advective form implements
a zero-gradient inflow condition at the lid: for `w < 0` the upwinded
top-face flux `w·χ_top` cancels the `χ_top·w` term exactly, equivalent to
prescribing `χ = χ_top` above the lid. For uniform `χ` the tendency vanishes
identically at every level, so tracer-mass (`χ ≡ 1`) consistency holds. 
This also consolidates all advective divergences into a
single operator (`ᶜadvdivᵥ`).

* Delete both `external_forcing.jl` blocks that zeroed the accumulated
`ρe_tot`/`ρq_tot` tendencies in the top cell; they existed to mask the
extrapolation problem and also discarded radiation/nudging/sponge
tendencies there. GCM/ERA5-driven cases will change as a result.

* Extend the tracer-mass consistency test with subsidence assertions:
top-cell tendency vanishes for descending flow with a non-constant
profile (fails at 4.2e-8 on the pre-fix operator), and the tendency
vanishes at every level for uniform `χ` under all reconstruction schemes.

This will affect calibration of SCM cases; they should be recalibrated. 

This goes on top of #4637.
title: Fix subsidence top BC and remove top-cell tendency-zeroing workaround
